definition: Use q-gram, partition, local-minimum, embedding, or prefix filters to generate candidate string pairs, then verify candidates with exact edit distance and emit pairs within a threshold or top-k most similar strings.

scale_profile.best_for: Large collections of short strings, identifiers, barcodes, or symbolic records where edit distance is the scientific similarity.
scale_profile.typical_upper_bound: Millions of strings in optimized or distributed join implementations.
scale_profile.avoid_when: Biological local alignment or homology is required; use sequence seed-and-extend instead.

tunable_parameters.name: partition_scheme
tunable_parameters.purpose: Controls exactness and pruning for partition-based joins.

quality_gates: Verify final distances with exact edit distance.
Report q-gram size, threshold, and string normalization.
Check recall against brute force on a small subset.
failure_modes: Unicode normalization or casing can change distances.
Long strings with large thresholds can create many candidates.
Edit distance may not capture semantic similarity in natural-language text.
example_usage: python string_qgram_edit_join.py --train train.csv --eval eval.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --text-col name --max-distance 2 --fallback-exact

citations.title: PASS-JOIN: A Partition-based Method for Similarity Joins
citations.evidence: Introduces a partition-based method with inverted indexes for edit-distance string similarity joins.

citations.title: MinJoin: Efficient Edit Similarity Joins via Local Hash Minima
citations.evidence: Uses local hash minima and string partitioning for efficient exact edit similarity joins.
